description_long: This is a vintage box-style television with a rectangular body finished in a faded, peach-pink color, showing signs of age and wear with scattered stains and dark smudges. The front features a convex glass screen displaying a classic test pattern, bordered by a thick, black bezel. To the right of the screen, there is a vertical panel with three round control knobs and a large speaker grille below them, all in black. Two thin, black metal antennas extend from a central mount on the top surface, giving the television a retro appearance. The back panel is mostly black with vent slats and a recessed area likely for ports or cable connections. The overall design is utilitarian, with sharp edges and a sturdy, boxy form typical of mid-to-late 20th-century televisions.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a peach-pink body, black front bezel, control knobs, speaker grille, and dual antennas. The screen displays a test pattern, and the exterior shows signs of wear and age.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the peach-pink surface with scattered stains and the central base of the two black antennas.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the convex glass screen with a test pattern, three control knobs, and a large speaker grille on the right.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the plain peach-pink side panel with visible stains and the protruding black back section.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the black back panel with vent slats and a recessed area, as well as the base of the antennas.
attribute_bezel_design: thick black plastic bezel with a utilitarian, rectangular shape and no decorative features
attribute_control_button_layout: three round knobs in a vertical row on the front right panel
